heroHeadline: Trump Extends Ceasefire Indefinitely; IRGC Seizes Three Ships in Hormuz as Iran Retaliates
heroSubtitle: President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire open-endedly on April 22 — citing Iran's 'seriously fractured' government — but kept the naval blockade fully in force and gave Iran 'days' to submit a unified proposal. VP JD Vance canceled his planned Islamabad trip after Iran refused to send a delegation. Hours after the extension, Iran's IRGC Navy seized cargo ships MSC Francesca and Epaminondas in the Strait of Hormuz and fired on a third vessel, the Euphoria, which ran aground on the Iranian coast. Brent crude spiked toward $99/barrel — near $100 — before pulling back in volatile trading. US officials disclosed an 'absolute fracture' inside Iran between civilian negotiators and IRGC hardliners under Gen. Ahmad Vahidi, with neither side having access to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. New US Treasury sanctions targeted 14 individuals and entities procuring weapons for Iran.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-22. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, ABC News, NBC News, PBS NewsHour, Xinhua, Euronews, UN News, Axios, CFR, Time, Washington Post, Washington Times.
